Collegedunia Team

Content Curator | Updated on - May 3, 2026

AIIMS Delhi cutoff is available for INI-CET, AIIMS-PG, AIIMS-MSc, AIIMS-Paramedical, NEET, AIIMS-BSc-Nursing, AIIMS-MBBS here. You can check category wise AIIMS Delhi cutoff for popular courses such as M.D, M.S, MDS, M.Ch, DM, MD, M.Sc, B.Sc, B.Optom, MBBS, B.Sc {Hons.} below.

AIIMS Delhi offers admission to M.D through INI-CET. For Admission in M.S courses, AIIMS Delhi accept INI-CET score. You can check the table below to know the accepted exams for courses offered by AIIMS Delhi (Cutoff Range below is across all categories).

Course Exam Cutoff
M.D INI-CET 2 - 27847
M.S INI-CET 12 - 1259
MDS INI-CET 1 - 76
M.Ch INI-CET 51
DM INI-CET 168 - 172
MD INI-CET 1729 - 3906
M.Sc AIIMS-MSc 2 - 392
B.Sc AIIMS-Paramedical 9 - 6525
MBBS NEET
48 - 785618 (All India)
B.Sc {Hons.} AIIMS-BSc-Nursing 95 - 19266

AIIMS Delhi INI-CET Cutoff 2026

Students can take admission in M.D, M.S, MDS, M.Ch, DM, MD programs at AIIMS Delhi based on INI-CET score. As per AIIMS Delhi INI-CET Cutoff 2026, the overall cutoff for M.D programs in Round 2 is 2 - 27847 across all categories.

  • For M.S, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 12 - 1259 across all categories.
  • For MDS, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 1 - 76 across all categories.
  • For M.Ch, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 51 across all categories.
  • For DM, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 168 - 172 across all categories.
  • For MD, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 1729 - 3906 across all categories.

M.D Medicine, M.S General Surgery, MDS Conservative Dentistry & Endodontics, M.Ch Neuro Surgery, DM Medicine & Microbiology, MD Lab Medicine, are the top choices for students appearing in INI-CET.

In 2026, M.D Medicine closing rank for OBC Category is 18 - 47 in Round 2.

  • For M.S General Surgery, The INI-CET closing rank 2026 for OBC is 152 in Round 2.
  • For MDS Conservative Dentistry & Endodontics, The INI-CET closing rank 2026 for OBC is 1 in Round 2.
  • For M.Ch Neuro Surgery, The INI-CET closing rank 2026 for General is 51 in Round 2.
  • For DM Medicine & Microbiology, The INI-CET closing rank 2026 for General is 168 - 172 in Round 2.
  • For MD Lab Medicine, The INI-CET closing rank 2026 for OBC is 3906 in Round 2.

You can check M.D Medicine, M.S General Surgery, MDS Conservative Dentistry & Endodontics, M.Ch Neuro Surgery, DM Medicine & Microbiology, MD Lab Medicine, category-wise closing rank for INI-CET below:

AIIMS Delhi INI-CET Cutoff Trends

Admission at AIIMS Delhi in M.D Programs in 2026 was closed at 27847 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 46885 across all categories.

  • Admission in M.S Programs in 2026 was closed at 1259 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 7582 across all categories.
  • Admission in MDS Programs in 2026 was closed at 76 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 634 across all categories.
  • Admission in M.Ch Programs in 2026 was closed at 51 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 269 across all categories.
  • Admission in DM Programs in 2026 was closed at 172 rank whereas closing rank in 2024 was 492 across all categories.
  • Admission in MD Programs in 2026 was closed at 3906 across all categories.

MBBS , is the top choice for students appearing in NEET.

Cutoff Summary: AIIMS Delhi

  • NEET
  • AIIMS MSc
  • INI CET
  • AIIMS Paramedical
  • AIIMS BSc Nursing

The NEET 2025 Cutoff for AIIMS Delhi presents the admission ranks for its M.B.B.S. program. For the General category, the closing rank for admission stood at 48. This represents the final rank after the last round of counselling for securing a seat in this highly sought-after course. For candidates from other categories, the cutoff ranks for the M.B.B.S. course vary.

The admission cutoff for the OBC category is in the range of 206 to 207. For the SC category, the cutoff is 644, and for the ST category, it is 1405. The cutoff for the EWS category for securing admission to the M.B.B.S. program is 254.

...Read More
 By Collegedunia’s Personal AI

AIIMS Delhi: 4 Answered Questions

What marks are required to get into AIIMS Delhi in the NEET UG 2023?

Vedant MishraMBBS from All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi

Being the dream college for all medical students, AIIMS Delhi has a strict admission process as it only filters the best into the college. As a result, the cutoffs are higher than other medical colleges and it’s difficult to secure a seat irrespective of the category you belong to. There is tough competition between the aspirants and the acceptance rate is low. 

Admission to AIIMS institutes is solely based on NEET scores. Those who clear the required cutoff are eligible for the admission process. Based on the recently concluded exam, the expected NEET cut-off of various categories for AIIMS MBBS admission is as follows

AIIMS Delhi cutoff 2023

Category 

NEET 2023 Cut off Percentile

NEET 2023 Cut off Marks (Expected)

General/EWS

50

720-130

OBC/SC/ST

40

129-104

OBC/ SC/ ST – PwD

40

114-95

General/ EWS- PwD

45

129-115

If you are a general student, you need to score at least 50 percentile in NEET-UG to get admission into AIIMS Delhi.

...Read More
0
0

What is the minimum marks needed to get admission into AIIMS Delhi for general category?

Ojas ReddyStudies at All India Institute of Medical Sciences (2024)

The minimum marks required to get admission into AIIMS Delhi vary from category to category.

  • General: 100 marks(50%) 
  • OBC: 90 marks (45%) 
  • SC/ST: 80 marks (40%)

Other factors that affect the requirement of marks are 

  • the difficulty of the question paper 
  • the number of seats available that year 

Based on the data of the past few years, it can be concluded that a score of 120+ increases your chances of admission to AIIMS Delhi.

...Read More
0
0

How many marks are required in the AIIMS 2022 to get a seat at AIIMS Delhi?

Aditya GhoseAll India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi

There’s no way to predict the certain marks that you get you a seat at AIIMS Delhi. Last year, the opening and closing rank for the AIQ seats were respectively, 1 and 53.  According to the trends of AIIMS cutoff 2022, the required percentile for the unreserved category was 98.83.

How many marks does an AIIMS topper Nishita Purohit get Also how many marks does one require to get admission in AIIMS Delhi expected for 2021?

Deepika VermaStudent at All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi (2017-present)

No one can tell the exact marks as AIIMS does not reveal the actual score of any person. It only releases a percentile. However, there are certain criteria for marks to get you in AIIMS Delhi.

A score of 145-150 is considered safe for admission to AIIMS New Delhi, whereas a score of 155-160 is considered the safest. With this score, your admission is guaranteed.

However, it is not easy because as competition is increasing day by day, only your hard work and dedication can get you in.
